name: Eloxx Pharmaceuticals, Inc.
business: Eloxx Pharmaceuticals is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing novel small-molecule product candidates designed to modulate the ribosome and promote readthrough of premature stop codons induced by nonsense mutations to enable production of full-length proteins. The company's immediate focus is advancing exaluren for rare kidney diseases (Alport syndrome and autosomal dominant polycystic kidney disease) and, through a collaboration with Almirall, ZKN-013 for rare skin diseases (recessive dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa and junctional epidermolysis bullosa).
sellsTo: Patients with nonsense-mutation-mediated rare kidney diseases (Alport syndrome, ADPKD)
Patients with nonsense-mutation-mediated rare skin diseases (RDEB, JEB)

exposures.sentence: We are a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing novel, small-molecule product candidates designed to modulate the ribosome and promote readthrough of premature stop codons induced by nonsense mutations (NMs) to enable the production of full-length proteins.

catalysts.event: Initiate Phase 2b clinical trial for exaluren in NMAS patients
catalysts.sentence: We plan to initiate a Phase 2b clinical trial in the first half of 2026 for exaluren in NMAS patients and anticipate topline data from the initial 16-week placebo-controlled part of the study by mid-2027 with the final readout by the end of 2027.

catalysts.event: Topline data from initial 16-week placebo-controlled part of Phase 2b exaluren study
catalysts.sentence: We plan to initiate a Phase 2b clinical trial in the first half of 2026 for exaluren in NMAS patients and anticipate topline data from the initial 16-week placebo-controlled part of the study by mid-2027 with the final readout by the end of 2027.

catalysts.event: Final readout of Phase 2b exaluren study
catalysts.sentence: We plan to initiate a Phase 2b clinical trial in the first half of 2026 for exaluren in NMAS patients and anticipate topline data from the initial 16-week placebo-controlled part of the study by mid-2027 with the final readout by the end of 2027.

catalysts.event: Initiate enrollment in Phase 2 trial of exaluren for nmADPKD
catalysts.sentence: We plan to initiate enrollment in a Phase 2 trial of exaluren for the treatment of nmADPKD in 2027 following protocol finalization and clearance of an Investigational New Drug application (IND) by the FDA.

catalysts.event: Topline data from Phase 2 trial of exaluren for nmADPKD
catalysts.sentence: We anticipate topline data from this trial by mid-2028.

tamClaims.claim: NMAS affects approximately 5,000 patients in the United States and approximately 14,000 patients in developed countries and China.
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate that NMAS affects roughly 6% to 7% of AS patients or approximately 5,000 patients in the United States and approximately 14,000 patients in developed countries and China.

tamClaims.claim: ADPKD affects approximately 160,000 to 200,000 patients in the United States.
tamClaims.sentence: ADPKD is the most common monogenic kidney disease based on genetic diagnosis, affecting approximately 160,000 to 200,000 patients in the United States.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 26% of ADPKD patients have NMs, resulting in approximately 40,000 to 50,000 patients in the United States.
tamClaims.sentence: Approximately 26% of ADPKD patients have NMs in the PKD1 and PKD2 genes resulting in a prevalence of approximately 40,000 to 50,000 patients in the United States.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 4,000 patients with NMs in RDEB and JEB in major markets.
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate that there are approximately 4,000 patients with NMs in these diseases in the major markets of the United States, Japan and Western Europe.
